block_00: Will you be trading Canadian GDP?
block_01: Statistical agencies from Canada will be releasing monthly GDP values on Friday, March 29 at 12:30 GMT time. Forecast stands at 0.1% compared to a previous negative number of -0.1%.
block_03: GDP stands for Gross Domestic Product and it’s the widest measurement that tracks the overall economic activity of a country. Higher numbers than forecasts tend to be good for currencies, in this case the Canadian Dollar.
block_05: Canadian GDP on March came in negative at -0.1% from a forecast of 0% growth. Markets reacted negatively on the news, as a result Canadian Dollar depreciated against the US Dollar.
block_06: Markets reacted aggressively to the report as 6 consecutive months of negative GDP indicates a recession!
